acceptance_criteria: Create a minimal, dependency-ordered M2 task DAG under project/tasks/active with explicit owner_role, reviewer_role, risk_level, dependencies, allowed_paths, forbidden_paths, acceptance_criteria, required_tests, route_to, and notes for every M2 task.

The M2 DAG covers Roadmap M2 goals without implementing them: 30 District/10 Settlement fixture, seasonal regional curves, agriculture/labor/population/grain/cash scaffolding, route/transport basics, map/list client pressure fixtures, content compiler bad-map/bad-reference rejection, and M2 exit validation.
The DAG schedules M1-GATE follow-ups: Chromium/Electron host hash canary before M2 exit and sim-core/content-runtime boot-boundary contract test during M2 world/content integration.
Tasks are small enough for independent writer/reviewer routing and do not create a single giant M2 implementation task.
No product code, package code, content-source data, docs/roadmap edits, frozen ADR changes, or M2 implementation work is performed by this task.
systems_architect writer and independent qa_reviewer reviewer produce validated handoffs before lead acceptance.
required_tests: node -e JSON validation for all new M2 task files and the systems handoff
notes: M2 is world, economy, and population vertical slice planning. Do not implement M2 functionality in this task.
M2 task graph must preserve frozen decisions in PROJECT_STATUS.md and M5 Human Gate policy.
Created a dependency-ordered M2 DAG with all downstream implementation and validation tasks left in DRAFT for lead selection after QA review.

Revision 1 addressed QA dependency findings: M2-CLIENT-MAP-001 now depends on both route/transport and economy/population scaffolding; M2-CLIENT-DISTRICT-LIST-001 now depends on both economy/population and route/transport scaffolding.
No product, package, content, roadmap, architecture, or implementation files were changed.
